FCF-REACT Food Security and Livelihoods Baseline-PDM Final
content:
Check out some of the latest findings of the baseline survey (including with post cash distribution monitoring questions) led by an external consultant to 70 households (10% of the total caseload):
  • 96% agree that the cash transfers are the most effective form of assistance to help their family;
  • 100% of households report that receiving cash assistance has helped to address child protection concerns for the child in their care;
  • 95% with same number of children agree that the cash transfer made it possible to keep their children within the caregiver by enabling children to study (46%), to have enough food (42%) or to not have to work (23%);
  • 4% claimed that they can fulfil all their basic needs, followed by 27% who can fulfil most of their basic needs whilst most (69%) can still only fulfil some of them. None of the respondents claimed that they were unable to fulfil any of their basic needs which is positive;
  • 99% reported they need support to restore existing/develop new livelihood activities

title: SCI Cambodia_Livelihood Assessment Report
content:
To what extent market-based approach may be integrated into the provision of case management services?
In an attempt to demystify the risks and opportunities that cash-based assistance may entail to reduce the risks of family separation, FCF-REACT partners are implementing a child-sensitive cash & livelihood assistance program aiming at mitigating the secondary impacts of COVID-19 and at achieving child protection outcomes.
Targeted households are already receiving case management, are at risk of family separation or have been recently reintegrated.
As part of this exciting journey, FCF-REACT partners have engaged in a livelihood assessment to better understand the socio-economic impacts of COVID-19 on their client households, their capabilities, their livelihood preferences and the barriers they may face to restore/develop livelihood activities.
